title: web-uplift project config
description: Optional per-project config (web-uplift.json) a developer drops at the root of the site being audited (or passes to the audit). It lets the developer declare scope, opt-outs and intent per principle WITH A REASON, so the audit can honour them and report 'opted-out' / 'not-applicable' distinctly from a real issue. This is the opt-in half of the 'quality without shaming' design: contextual principles (be-resilient/offline, be-internationalised, be-agent-ready, the absolute weight bar of be-sustainable, public discoverability) may legitimately not apply, and a declared opt-out with a reason is respected rather than penalised. The config NEVER suppresses a default-expectation principle silently: a declared opt-out is recorded and shown in the report.

siteType.description: Free-form hint about what the site is (e.g. 'static brochure site', 'real-time collaborative editor', 'single-locale internal tool'). Helps the model judge contextual principles proportionally.

scope.description: Optional declaration of what to audit.

routes.description: Specific routes/paths the developer wants audited (otherwise the model recons and chooses).

sourceDir.description: Path to the local source, equivalent to --source.

optOut.description: Map of principleId (or principleId/checkId) -> reason string. A declared opt-out is reported as 'opted-out' (with the reason) and NOT counted as an issue. Use only for principles whose applicability is 'contextual', or for a check that genuinely does not apply to this site; opting out of a 'default' principle is recorded and surfaced so it cannot hide a real gap.

additionalProperties.description: Why this principle/check does not apply to this site.

examples.be-resilient: static brochure site, offline not needed
examples.be-agent-ready: no agent-facing surface; not exposing WebMCP yet
examples.be-internationalised: single-locale internal tool, one language only

intent.description: Map of principleId -> a statement of the developer's intent for that principle, so the model judges against what the team is actually trying to do rather than a generic bar. Does not suppress findings; it sets context.

examples.be-sustainable: media product; large hero video is intentional but everything else should be lean
examples.maximize-content-reduce-noise: the cookie banner is legally required in our jurisdiction

guidanceCatalogVersion.description: Override the Modern Web Guidance catalog version to consult (defaults to the version pinned in knowledge/principles.json).
